name: Spiced Apple Pie
recipeIngredient: Unsalted Butter (chilled): 1/2 cup, cut into small pieces
Vegetable Shortening (solid): 1/2 cup, chilled and cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
-------------
Filling:
- Green Apples: 3 pounds, peeled and cut into 1/4-inch slices
- Sugar (for apples): 1/2 cup + 1 teaspoon
- Golden Brown Sugar: 1/2 cup, packed
- All Purpose Flour: 1/4 cup
- Lemon Juice: 1 tablespoon fresh
- Ground Cinnamon: 1/2 teaspoon
- Ground Nutmeg: 1/2 teaspoon
- Grated Lemon Peel: 1/2 teaspoon
- Ground Mace: 1/2 teaspoon
- Ground Cloves: 1/2 teaspoon
- Ground Allspice: 1/4 teaspoon
- Milk: 1 tablespoon
Crust:
1. Combine flour, sugar, salt, and mace in a food processor.
2. Using on/off turns, cut in chilled butter and vegetable shortening until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
3. Gradually blend in enough water by tablespoonfuls to form moist clumps.
4. Gather dough into a ball. Divide dough into 2 pieces.
5. Flatten each piece into a disk. Wrap each in plastic and refrigerate for 1 hour (can be prepared up to 3 days ahead; keep refrigerated; soften dough slightly at room temperature before rolling).
Assembly:
- Preheat oven to 400°F.
- In a large bowl, toss apples with 1/2 cup sugar, brown sugar, flour, lemon juice, cinnamon, nutmeg, lemon peel, mace, cloves, and allspice.
- Roll out one dough disk on a lightly floured surface to a 12-inch diameter round. Transfer to a 9-inch deep-dish glass pie plate. Trim dough overhang to 1/2".
- Brush edge of crust lightly with water.
- Transfer apple mixture to the crust, mounding in the center.
- Roll out second dough disk to a 12-inch diameter round. Place atop apples. Trim dough overhang to 1"; reserve scraps.
- Fold top crust edge under bottom crust edge, pressing to seal. Decoratively crimp edges.
- Roll pastry scraps and cut out leaf shapes. Brush one side of each leaf with milk and arrange, milk side down, decoratively atop the crust.
- Cut several slits in the crust to allow steam to escape. Brush crust with milk; sprinkle with remaining 1 teaspoon sugar.
- Place pie on a baking sheet. Bake until crust is golden brown and juices bubble (covering crust edges with aluminum foil if browning too quickly), about 1 hour 10 minutes. Transfer pie to a rack and cool.
Source: Bon Appetit Magazine - November, 1994
